content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 28, 1993


Dunham Reilly
451 Country Club Lane
Coronado, California 92118

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-016

Dear Mr. Reilly:

     This is in response to your request for informal advice
relating to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

     You are requesting advice regarding a possible conflict of
interest involving members of the city council of the City of
Coronado and the city's traffic plan decisions. While the Act
requires that the Commission provide formal written advice to any
person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that
person's authorized representative, the Commission will decline to
provide advice to third parties concerning another person's duties
under the Act. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(b)(8).)

     Furthermore, as I indicated to you in our telephone
conversation of January 26, 1993, the questions posed by your
letter involve issues of past conduct. We do not provide advice
regarding past conduct, unless the advice or assistance sought is
related to possible amendment of reports previously filed by the
person requesting the advice. (Regulation 18329(c)(4).) That
does not appear to be the case here.

     In your letter of January 8, 1993, you also ask about
apparent Brown Act violations. As I indicated to you, this area

_______________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. Informal
assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity
provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114;
Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. 93-016
Page 2


of the law is not within the Commission's jurisdiction.  You may
wish to contact the Attorney General's Office regarding that
issue.

You stated in our telephone conversation that you intended to
file a complaint with the Commission.  Therefore, it will forward
your letters of January 4, 1993, and January 8, 1993, to the
Commission's Enforcement Division.

If you have any questions, please contact the Commission at
(916) 322-5901.2

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner,
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature: Luisa Menchaca]
                                        By:  Luisa Menchaca
                                             Counsel, Legal Division




LM:docs:93016




_________________________
2  Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Does a possible conflict of interest exist involving members of the Coronado city council regarding the city's traffic plan decisions, and are there Brown Act violations?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide informal advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act, and does not provide advice regarding past conduct unless related to report amendments. Brown Act violations are outside the Commission's jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter formatted as correspondence. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Content indicates request for informal assistance that FPPC cannot provide under stated regulations. Document includes explanatory citations and procedural information about complaint referral.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a possible conflict of interest exist involving members of the Coronado city council regarding the city's traffic plan decisions, and are there Brown Act violations?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide informal advice to third parties concerning another person's duties under the Act, and does not provide advice regarding past conduct unless related to report amendments. Brown Act violations are outside the Commission's jurisdiction.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide informal advice to Dunham Reilly regarding alleged conflicts of interest involving Coronado city council members' traffic plan decisions, citing restrictions on third-party requests and past conduct advice. The letter forwards Reilly's complaints to the Enforcement Division and notes that Brown Act issues fall outside FPPC jurisdiction.
